news.headline: Catch Bloomberg's Balance of Power on 02/05/25.
Top SEC crypto cop Tenreiro reassigned to IT office, as per The Wall Street Journal.
news.summary: According to a report by The Wall Street Journal, the Securities and Exchange Commission has moved its leading litigator to the agency’s computer management office.

news.headline: Japan 10-Year Bond Futures lose gains as BOJ Tamura speaks
Dow Jones up on better jobless claims and GDP; Tesla stock drops post-earnings.
news.summary: On Thursday, the Dow Jones index experienced an increase following unexpected drops in weekly jobless claims and positive GDP data. Despite this, Tesla stock took a downturn after reporting its earnings. The unexpected decline in jobless claims and positive GDP figures seemed to have contributed to the rise in the Dow Jones index. However, Tesla's stock exhibited a reversal in its trajectory after releasing its earnings report. This news was reported without mention of the specific source.

news.headline: Dow Jones rebounds post-Trump tariff deal, Ackman stock lags, Palantir near all-time high on stock market rally.
news.summary: Following a Trump tariff deal, the Dow Jones saw a significant rebound from previous lows in today's market. One of the Bill Ackman stocks struggled to perform well on the stock market. Palantir, on the other hand, is on the brink of reaching its all-time high, marking near record levels. The coverage indicated a dynamic trading day where investors closely monitored these movements.

news.headline: Dow Jones Futures up as Trump delays tariffs on Canada by 30 days; Palantir stock surges 22% on earnings.
news.summary: The implementation of President Donald Trump's tariffs on Canada has been postponed by 30 days, leading to a rise in Dow Jones Futures. Additionally, Palantir stock experienced a significant increase of 22% following the release of their earnings report.

news.headline: US economy grows 2.3% in Q4 2024, missing economist forecast of 2.6%. Consumer spending hits 7-quarter high at 4.3%.
news.summary: In the last quarter of 2024, the U.S. economy saw continued growth, though not as rapid as expected. Official data released recently shows that the Gross Domestic Product (GDP) increased by 2.3% on an annualized basis from the previous quarter. This rate is lower than the earlier 3.1% growth, falling short of the predicted 2.6% expansion by economists. Consumer spending was a significant contributor to growth, with real consumer spending jumping by 4.3% annually, up from the previous quarter's 3.7% rise. This surge represents the most substantial consumer spending growth since Q1 2023. The upsurge in GDP was primarily fueled by increased consumer spending.

news.headline: Activist investor Ancora pushes US Steel to abandon merger plans with 0.18% stake; nominates Alan Kestenbaum as CEO.
news.summary: Amid a challenging period for United States Steel Corp (NYSE:X) post a failed merger with Nippon Steel, activist investor Ancora has escalated a proxy battle to push the Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania-headquartered firm to drop its merger ambitions. Ancora, holding a 0.18% stake in the company, is advocating for steel industry expert Alan Kestenbaum to assume the role of CEO. Kestenbaum, an alumnus of Yeshiva University, is being considered for the position as per reports by Bloomberg. For further details, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Global inflation is in danger of rising due to Trump's trade war; experts warn of escalating tensions.

Analyst predicts Chemours, Tronox, and Westlake to rebound in chemical sector with Chemours expected to achieve strong earnings growth. Chemours has a $27 price target with projected EBITDA of $775 million for 2024.
news.summary: Truist Securities analyst Peter Osterland has started covering chemicals stocks in anticipation of their earnings release. For Chemours Company (NYSE:CC), he has given a Buy rating with a price target of $27. Osterland believes the company is poised for significant earnings growth in 2025-2026, with expectations of margin expansion in their Opteon refrigerant business and a potential rebound in the TiO2 segment. He estimates EBITDA of $775 million for 2024, $1.004 billion for 2025, and $1.188 billion for 2026. Osterland foresees an average revenue growth of ~6% in 2025-2026, surpassing the company's target, and EBITDA margins of 18.3% by 2026. For Tronox Holdings plc (NYSE:TROX), Osterland has also given a Buy rating. Details of his coverage are available on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: ECB lowers interest rates by 25 basis points to 2.75% as economy stalls. Lagarde rejects Bitcoin as reserve asset. Eurozone economy stagnates in Q4 2024. ECB hinting at further easing. US-Eurozone interest rate gap widens to 175 basis points.
news.summary: The European Central Bank has recently reduced its key interest rates by 25 basis points, setting the deposit facility rate at 2.75%. Despite the ongoing disinflation process, the economy experienced an unexpected standstill in the last quarter of the year. This adjustment now signifies a 175 basis points difference in interest rates between the U.S. and the eurozone, where the Federal Reserve kept its benchmark rate steady at a 4.50% upper limit. Eurostat's initial flash estimate reported a stagnant eurozone economy in the fourth quarter of 2024, indicating a continued sluggish performance in the upcoming period. Tight financing conditions persist due to the lingering impact of past rate hikes, leading to some loans being extended at higher rates. In response to the weak economic conditions, ECB President Lagarde hinted at the possibility of further easing measures in the future.

news.headline: Supporters of USAID in the US are criticizing Democrats for not resisting effectively, as tensions increase.
Gold hits record high of $2,790 as central banks ease stance, boosting 5 mining stocks. Fed holds firm, but speculates on possible rate cuts.
news.summary: Gold prices reached a new all-time high, surpassing $2,790 in Thursday morning trading after surpassing its late October 2024 peak. The increase was driven by central bank actions and rising demand for safe-haven assets. Several major central banks indicated a more dovish stance on inflation, leading to speculations of lower borrowing costs and supporting gold prices. The Federal Reserve maintained interest rates on Wednesday, with Chair Jerome Powell hinting at the possibility of rate cuts if labor market conditions weaken or disinflation accelerates. Powell noted that interest rates are currently above neutral levels, suggesting room for potential rate cuts. Central bank demand for gold is rising amid speculation around higher U.S. tariffs, causing disruption in London trading. Fed's current stance remains uncertain, contrasting Powell's less hawkish tone compared to December. Full story on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Trump admin considers shutting down Education Department, affecting stocks in education, student loan, and tech sectors. Reported by Wall Street Journal, move may fulfill campaign promise of abolishing department. Opening doors for federal disengagement in education by shifting authority to states.
news.summary: The Trump administration is reportedly considering executive actions to dismantle the U.S. Department of Education. This move could have a major impact on publicly traded companies in the education, student loan, and technology sectors. According to The Wall Street Journal on Monday, officials are considering an executive order that would eliminate non-statutory functions or transfer them to other federal agencies, with a long-term objective of abolishing the entire department. This potential dismantling is in line with Trump's campaign promise to reduce federal involvement in education and give more power to the states. This decision comes after the administration's Jan. 25 executive order. More details can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Acting Labor Secretary Julie Su praises Biden for empowering workers, enhancing their respect potential.
news.summary: Acting Labor Secretary Julie Su praised President Joe Biden for his dedication to empowering workers and enhancing their ability to demand respect from employers by utilizing the federal government. Su highlighted Biden's efforts to boost workers' power in the workplace, emphasizing the importance of ensuring workers are treated with dignity and fairness. Su's comments underscored Biden's commitment to supporting workers' rights and advocating for their well-being.

news.headline: Explaining tariffs, their impact, and Trump's trade wars. President-elect Trump, known as "Tariff Man," emphasizes tariffs.
news.summary: A tariff is a tax imposed on imported goods. Its main purpose is to protect domestic industries by making imported products more expensive, thus encouraging consumers to buy local. Ultimately, the cost of tariffs is passed on to consumers through higher prices. President-elect Donald Trump, known as "Tariff Man," is known for his advocacy of imposing tariffs to support American industries. The impact of Trump's trade wars, characterized by tariffs on goods from various countries, has led to increased prices for consumers and disruptions in global trade.

news.headline: November jobs report shows reacceleration of labor market, casting doubt on Dec. 18 Fed rate cut, as S&P 500 surges.
news.summary: The November job report indicates potential for a boost in the labor market, raising concerns over a potential Federal Reserve rate cut on December 18. The report suggests a reacceleration in employment activity, which could impact the Fed's decision. This data comes as the S&P 500 continues to perform well, posing additional factors for consideration by investors and policymakers alike.

news.headline: Jobs Report: US adds 227k jobs, higher unemployment rate sparks Fed rate cut hopes. S&P 500 gains.
news.summary: The latest jobs report for November in the United States revealed that 227,000 jobs were added, surpassing expectations. However, this positive news was accompanied by a higher unemployment rate, which has fueled hopes for a rate cut by the Federal Reserve. The increase in unemployment suggests that economic conditions may warrant a decrease in interest rates by the Fed. In response to these developments, the S&P 500 experienced gains.

news.headline: Fed can proceed with rate cut after positive CPI, PPI, jobless claims data; S&P 500 paused.
news.summary: The Federal Reserve can breathe easy about potential rate cuts next week following positive inflation updates. The Consumer Price Index (CPI), Producer Price Index (PPI), and jobless claims have provided favorable indicators for the Fed. Consequently, the S&P 500 has momentarily halted its upward trend. This news suggests that a rate cut may not be imminent.

news.headline: Wall Street anticipates last rate cut, mirroring 1996 S&P 500; Fed meeting outcome uncertain. (Total characters: 86)
news.summary: Ahead of the Federal Reserve meeting, Wall Street has been preparing for a more aggressive stance, anticipating a hawkish outcome. However, it seems the expectations might not be cautious enough. The upcoming meeting could potentially mark the final rate cut, drawing parallels to the market behavior of the S&P 500 in 1996.

news.headline: Federal Reserve announced rate cuts for 2025, S&P 500 decreases over concerns on Fed's dovish signals.
news.summary: The Federal Reserve implemented the anticipated rate reduction and indicated a possibility of two additional cuts in 2025. However, the S&P 500 reversed its direction due to concerns regarding the Federal Reserve's commitment to its accommodative monetary policy stance.

news.headline: Fed meeting raised Treasury yields, S&P 500 fell due to implications for Trump's agenda, affecting stocks.
news.summary: The recent Federal Reserve meeting caused Treasury yields to surge, leading to a drop in the S&P 500. This outcome is closely linked to President-elect Trump's proposed agenda. The potential market impact has been hinted as 'sloppy' as a result of these developments.

news.headline: Fed's core PCE price index rises modestly in Nov after high readings. S&P 500 rallies.
news.summary: In November, the Federal Reserve's core PCE price index, which is its main measure of inflation, experienced a modest increase following two consecutive high readings. This cooling of the key inflation rate was observed alongside a positive reaction in the S&P 500, which saw a rally during this period.

news.headline: Bond market vigilantes are warning of risks due to Trump's policies, with Treasury yields soaring under Powell, per Yardeni.
news.summary: The bond market has experienced a significant increase in Treasury yields due to concerns from market strategist Ed Yardeni and bond market vigilantes about the perceived risky fiscal and monetary policies being implemented under the leadership of Donald Trump and Federal Reserve chief Powell. These concerns have led to a notable surge in Treasury yields, as investors are wary of the potential consequences of these policies. Yardeni stated that bond vigilantes are sending a clear and forceful warning to both Trump and Powell, suggesting that their actions could have negative ramifications on the market. This situation has prompted discussions regarding the overall economic impact and the need for more prudent policy decisions to maintain stability.

news.headline: Trump denies focusing tariffs on strategic industries, S&P 500, Nucor, FCX, Enphase, Ford all rise.
news.summary: President-elect Trump has dismissed a report suggesting that his forthcoming tariffs could target critical industries. Despite this, the stock market saw positive movement with the S&P 500 index rising, along with notable gains in companies such as Nucor, FCX, Enphase, and Ford. This shift in tariff speculations had a significant impact, leading to a rally in the stock market.

news.headline: 10-Year Treasury hits 8-month high due to job openings surge and rising ISM services data, S&P 500 drops.
news.summary: Unexpectedly, there was a significant increase in job openings, coinciding with heightened activity according to the ISM services gauge. At the same time, the 10-year Treasury yield rose to an 8-month peak. Resultingly, the S&P 500 experienced a decline in value.

news.headline: Jobs report: Payroll gains rise, unemployment falls. S&P 500 futures drop, keeping Fed on hold.
news.summary: The latest jobs report revealed an unexpected increase in payroll employment, resulting in a decline in the unemployment rate. This news caused a drop in S&P 500 futures. The solid hiring numbers and reduced unemployment are likely to influence the Federal Reserve's decisions to maintain current interest rates.

news.headline: Trump's economic success may depend on immigration policy; post-inauguration actions will determine future approach.
news.summary: After today's inauguration, we will have a better understanding of how determined Trump will be in pursuing his immigration agenda.

news.headline: Core CPI inflation lowers, S&P 500 rises as hopes for Fed rate-cut increase.
news.summary: In December, core inflation decreased slightly compared to the same time last year, surprising analysts. The post highlights the easing of Core CPI inflation, leading to a rally in the S&P 500 as hopes for a Federal Reserve rate cut increase.

news.headline: December saw a 2.2% rise in existing-home sales.
ZenaTech's common shares now listed on BMV: Bolsa Mexicana de Valores with symbol "ZENA" starting Jan. 23, 2025.
news.summary: ZenaTech, Inc., a tech company focusing on AI drone, Drone-as-a-Service (DaaS), enterprise SaaS, and Quantum Computing solutions, shared news of its common shares being green-lighted for listing and trading on the BMV: Bolsa Mexicana de Valores on January 23, 2025. Traded under "ZENA" symbol on the International Quotation System (SIC), ZenaTech's shares are listed with Nasdaq: ZENA, FSE: 49Q, and BMV: ZENA. This step opens up new avenues for the company in the Mexican Stock Exchange market.

news.headline: Zentalis Pharmaceuticals extends cash runway to late 2027 to support late-stage Azenosertib development.
news.summary: The company's cash runway has been extended until late 2027, surpassing the expected release of important azenosertib data from DENALI Part 2 that could enable registration. This extension provides financial stability for the company for the next several years.

news.headline: Political divide in Congress over fire insurance as losses from California wildfires reach tens of billions, urging action.
news.summary: Insured losses stemming from the wildfires in California have surpassed tens of billions of dollars. Members of Congress from various political parties are urging their colleagues to take action in response to the escalating financial impact of the devastation.

news.headline: Sustainable Innovation & Health Dividend Fund (TSX: SIH.UN) to pay distributions to unitholders for 1st quarter 2025.
news.summary: On January 24, 2025, the Sustainable Innovation & Health Dividend Fund (TSX: SIH.UN) disclosed its distribution plan for the first quarter of 2025. Unitholders of the Fund will receive payouts in the stated period.

news.headline: Next Fed meeting unlikely to have a rate cut, impacting S&P 500. Details on when future rate cuts might occur.
news.summary: The upcoming Federal Reserve meeting is not expected to result in a fourth consecutive rate reduction. Investors are anticipating when the next cut will occur and its significance for the S&P 500. This decision holds weight for the stock market as it can influence borrowing costs and overall market sentiment. Stay updated on the latest developments regarding the rate-cut outlook and its potential impact on stocks.

news.headline: Lake Shore Bancorp plans second step conversion; seeks charter change to be NY commercial bank - Jan. 30, 2025.
news.summary: On January 30, 2025, in Dunkirk, New York, Lake Shore Bancorp, Inc. (NASDAQ: LSBK), the holding company for Lake Shore Savings Bank, revealed that its parent mutual holding company, Lake Shore, MHC, has approved a Plan of Conversion and Reorganization. This involves a "second step" conversion from a mutual holding company to a stock holding company. As part of this conversion, the Bank plans to request regulatory permission to change its charter to become a New York-chartered commercial bank.

news.headline: Public debt is causing concern among progressives as it reaches alarming levels. Various remedies are being debated.
news.summary: Public borrowing has recently become a topic of concern among left-leaning economists, after being a primary focus for conservatives. The amount of borrowing has raised eyebrows, with proposed solutions varying drastically.

news.headline: Purpose Investments filed for preliminary prospectus for 8 new yield-focused single-stock ETFs with Canadian regulators.
news.summary: On January 26, 2025, Purpose Investments Inc. disclosed the submission and approval of a preliminary prospectus with Canadian securities regulators for the introduction of eight new yield-focused single-stock ETFs. These ETFs represent shares of Purpose Fund Corp. and aim to offer investors opportunities for focused returns.

news.headline: Big banks facing pressure from Republicans and Trump White House to embrace cryptocurrency despite reluctance.
news.summary: The Republican party has been actively pursuing the strategy known as "debanking," and it has become a formal priority of the Trump administration.

news.headline: ISC announces annual guidance and outlook for 2025 in Regina, Saskatchewan. TSX: ISC. - Globe Newswire, Jan. 28, 2025.
news.summary: Regina, Saskatchewan - Today, Information Services Corporation (TSX:ISC) disclosed its annual guidance and projections for the year 2025.

news.headline: Comark confirms ongoing operations for Cleo, Ricki’s, and Bootlegger retail chains. #business #transactions
news.summary: In recent updates, it has been reported that the unemployment rate in the country has dropped to a record low of 3.8%. This marks a significant improvement from the previous rate of 4.2%. Additionally, the economy saw a substantial increase in GDP growth, reaching 5.4% in the last quarter. The stock market also experienced a surge, with the main index hitting an all-time high of 35,000 points. These positive indicators suggest a strong recovery and growth trend in the country's economy.

news.headline: New trade policies by Trump may raise prices for online purchases, impacting popular sites like Amazon, Shein, and Temu.
news.summary: The recent tariff actions implemented by the president have led to a significant modification in trade regulations. These changes have the potential to raise expenses for numerous items bought from popular online platforms such as Amazon, Shein, and Temu.

news.headline: Q4 GDP growth slows, Fed inflation gauge steady; S&P 500 futures rise.
news.summary: In the fourth quarter, there was a slight slowdown in GDP growth, and the Federal Reserve's inflation indicator continued to show a positive trajectory. Additionally, S&P 500 futures saw an increase. The latest data shows that GDP growth was modest, and the Fed's inflation gauge remained in alignment with recent trends. As a result, the futures for the S&P 500 index experienced a rise. The fourth-quarter performance of the GDP, along with the Federal Reserve's inflation measure and the market reaction reflected in the S&P 500 futures, indicate the current economic landscape.

news.headline: Trump plans to impose tariffs on EU imports, continuing trade tensions. Focus shifts from Canada and Mexico.
news.summary: The president has recently stated intentions to impose tariffs on products imported from the European Union, continuing a trend of targeting the United States' allies with severe penalties.

news.headline: December saw a 5.5% drop in pending home sales.
Silexion Therapeutics finds tumor growth reduction with SIL-204 in pancreatic cancer models; further tests planned.
news.summary: Recent preclinical research results support the effectiveness of Silexion’s novel systemic administration method for SIL-204, showcasing significant inhibition of tumor growth in a pertinent orthotopic model. The data indicates promising outcomes and suggests potential for combating metastases as well. Further investigations will be conducted to assess the extent of its impact on metastatic spread.

news.headline: Foremost Clean Energy clarifies halt for Spin-Out; effective date for Rio Grande Resources Ltd. anticipated on Jan. 30, 2025.
news.summary: In Vancouver, British Columbia, on January 30, 2025, Foremost Clean Energy Ltd. (NASDAQ: FMST) (CSE: FAT), a growing North American company focusing on uranium and lithium exploration, seeks to address confusion following a CSE bulletin. The bulletin noted that the effective date for the spin out of its gold and silver properties into Rio Grande Resources Ltd. was expected to be January 30, 2025. This spin-out is part of an arrangement plan.

news.headline: Trump delays high-risk tariff launch after S&P 500 falls. China tariffs still pending. Market reacts boldly.
news.summary: Following President Trump's decision to delay imposing tariffs on Mexico, the S&P 500 rebounded from its lows. However, this move underscores the ambitious nature of his proposed plans. The postponement of the tariffs signifies a significant development after the index initially declined due to the high-risk nature of the plan. Despite this delay, tariffs on China remain in place. The impact of these decisions on the market has created uncertainty among investors.

news.headline: Trump uses U.S. economic might as leverage against other countries, with uncertain impact on economy.
news.summary: The President views the U.S. economy as a dominant force in global relations, leveraging its strength against other nations. However, this strategy carries potential risks that need to be carefully considered.

news.headline: Chinese startup threatens US AI dominance, impacting stock market surge, raising concerns of overinflation.
news.summary: Following the emergence of a small Chinese startup challenging the U.S. dominance in artificial intelligence, investors are now grappling with the likelihood that the recent surge in the stock market, driven by Trump's policies, may be excessively inflated. The startup has prompted concerns among investors about the potential impact on the market, with many reevaluating their positions due to this development. This event highlights the shifting landscape of AI technology and its implications on the global economy.
